introSection: Project Zero is a Maurer Rides sit-down roller coaster currently located at Gumbuya World in Tynong, Victoria, Australia. It was previously located at Dreamworld in Coomera, Queensland, where it operated as BuzzSaw.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
BuzzSaw opened at Dreamworld on 17 September 2011.

In July 2021, Dreamworld announced that BuzzSaw would close on 31 August. The ride was dismantled in November 2021. It was later sent to Gumbuya World.

The ride reopened as Project Zero at Gumbuya World on 23 December 2022.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride beguins with a vertical lift hill leading out of the station. Upon reaching the top, rather than leveling off at 0° like most roller coasters, it levels off in the opposite direction, turning riders upside-down. The train then traveles through a barrel roll which leads into the vertical drop back into the station. Continuing through the station, the ride traveles back up the lift hill, before losing momentum and traveling backwards through the ride. It then loses momentum in the opposite direction and travels back into the station. This process is completed twice during the duration of the ride.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Single train with 2 cars. Riders are arranged 2 across in 3 rows for a total of 12 riders.

fullText:
Project Zero is a Maurer Rides sit-down roller coaster currently located at Gumbuya World in Tynong, Victoria, Australia. It was previously located at Dreamworld in Coomera, Queensland, where it operated as BuzzSaw.

BuzzSaw opened at Dreamworld on 17 September 2011.

In July 2021, Dreamworld announced that BuzzSaw would close on 31 August. The ride was dismantled in November 2021. It was later sent to Gumbuya World.

The ride reopened as Project Zero at Gumbuya World on 23 December 2022.

The ride beguins with a vertical lift hill leading out of the station. Upon reaching the top, rather than leveling off at 0° like most roller coasters, it levels off in the opposite direction, turning riders upside-down. The train then traveles through a barrel roll which leads into the vertical drop back into the station. Continuing through the station, the ride traveles back up the lift hill, before losing momentum and traveling backwards through the ride. It then loses momentum in the opposite direction and travels back into the station. This process is completed twice during the duration of the ride.

Single train with 2 cars. Riders are arranged 2 across in 3 rows for a total of 12 riders.
